other_observers: Joey Kellner, and maybe others I don't know about; first found by Joey Kellner
description: the bird was a large loon, slightly larger than Common Loon, though it was never seen in direct comparison with that species. The bird had a blocky head with a large lump above the eye. The bird would hold it's neck stretched, and thus looked "taller" than I would expect from a Common Loon. The bill was yellow, and the culmen was straight. There was a strong gonydeal angle about halfway along the lower mandible. The bird for the most part held its bill at a slight upturn.  The bird's head was a pale straw color, with a darker cap and back of the neck, and a fairly bold darker spot behind the eye. The throat and chest were white, and there was a white crescent above the eye, which when combined with the white throat created a somewhat "blank" look. There were two small white indentations into the pale straw color on the neck.  The back and sides of the bird were a medium brown, with bold pale barring along the back. Overall the bird appeared much paler than Common Loons elsewhere on the reservoir.Behaviors: the bird would spend its time out in the middle of the southern portion of the reservoir, preening and diving.Call: none heardPlumage: juvenal
